queryable_facts.answer: I am a 21-year-old male college student, and I hope I have finally learned my lesson. I typically get sunburnt pretty bad once a year, and then I no longer forget to use sunscreen. It is currently day three if you include the initial pool day, and it is not the worst burn I've ever had; it has become less red/painful every day, and no blisters, cracking or peeling. However, today I started feeling a pins and needles sensation, which I have never experienced from a burn. The feeling has lasted all day and has yet to improve, and the only thing that seems to provide relief is a cold shower or sitting in front of my A/C vent. I find it odd because a year or two ago, I occasionally began to experience the pins and needles sensation when I walked outside, got into my car on a hot day, or taking a hot shower, with the latter being almost unnoticeable compared to what I am currently feeling. Right now, I can't help but wonder if the flashes of Pins and needles on a typical hot day are a sign of skin damage or maybe even permanent nerve damage.

queryable_facts.answer: I am (or was) fully healthy, I don't take medications and I was physically active up until mid-November when I was struck with a rather harsh flu and I haven't made my debut back to the gym yet. From what I've learned online, the symptoms are rather simple: weakness or paralysis on one side of the body, eyesight problems (such as double vision), confusion, slurred speech and some degree of mental impairment, among others. What I'm feeling, however, is a sort of "weakness" and lack of coordination on my right arm, to the point of mistyping and misclicking a bit more frequently. I've even pinched my arm and fingers a couple of times to test my sensorial abilities there and all seems normal. The grip strength is around 80% of my left arm's but I'm left handed, so it's okay. Nevertheless, I can't help but feel some sort of awkwardness in my right arm, especially on the pinky. Also, if I truly had a stroke, would it be wise to get back to the gym?

queryable_facts.answer: About 6 months ago I started to have these strange bad feelings and things happening that I am so confused as to the reason. I'm 19m, 160 lbs. / 5'10 / white / don't drink or smoke, and take medically prescribed Adderall and testosterone and have been on both for about 1.5 years. I've worked out consistently for about 2 years and have maintained a pretty good diet constantly watching calories and protein along with staying hydrated, but now when weight training or even doing a push up I shake like its this hard thing I have not done 100 times. Someone asked me yesterday what my birthday was and I had to take 30 seconds to remember, it was like I just couldn't pull it out of my memory, same with things like dogs names, and very simple easy to remember things its like Ive forgotten them. In the shower yesterday I thought about my age randomly and honestly couldn't remember if I was 18 or 19 like I had to like use evidence to remember it as silly as that sounds to confirm and convince myself. queryable_facts.answer: Sudden urine incontinence I, 25F, suddenly cannot hold urine at all and it's freaking me out. I have never had serious problems with my urine (except a kidney stone last year) and a few bacteria here and there, but nothing big. Never officially diagnosed with an UTI, despite testing every few months. Some of my lab reports have shown high levels of albumin, protein and creatinine but when repeated, a few days later, my values were normal. I drink a lot of water everyday, probably around 1.5L and coffee once or twice/day for 10 years, I don't take any medication except vitatime D (for a couple of years now) and follow a strict gluten free diet, so nothing has changed in my eating/drinking habits. It all started a few weeks ago. I've always been on the "weaker bladder side" since I was a kid but never like this. I started waking every night to urinate around 3-5 am with a huge urge and again at 6 before work with an urge like I hadn't peed for days. During the day, I'd pee at least 10-12 times & every time it looks like a morning pee. The frequency of needing to urinate does not worry me because I've always peed 7-8 times/day but the lack of the ability to hold it, does. Suddenly I cannot hold it more than a few seconds, especially at night.

opening_utterance: I got my blood drawn yesterday and I think something is very wrong I 18f ( 5’5”, 140Ibs, mixed(Caucasian, African American) no existing medical conditions, no medications, no drugs or alcohol) went to the doctors office and had blood labs ordered, (all my blood labs came back normal today) The nurse comes in sets everything up and tells me to distract myself so I get in my phone but as soon as the needle goes in it hurts very badly, 8/10 on a pain scale, she says she’s having a hard time finding my vain and keeps moving the needle around in my arm and at this point I’m hyperventilating, sweating, and shaking and I keep telling her it hurts really bad, she told me to just take deep breaths and that I was doing fine and she still hasn’t gotten any blood until she takes off the band around my arm she told me she wasn’t getting a lot of blood and after about 45 more seconds stops and takes the needle out because there wasn’t any more blood flow and she said she didn’t want me to pass out from hyperventilating, she didnt get enough blood so she had to draw from my other arm which hurt significantly less, I’ve had my blood drawn before and I barley felt it.
